---
title: "Introduction"
subtitle: "Supervised vs. unsupervised learning"
date: "Aug 28, 2026"
date-format: long
---

Course overview and the landscape of statistical learning. We distinguish supervised problems (regression, classification) from unsupervised ones (clustering, dimensionality reduction), and motivate the rest of the semester through health-science examples.

📖 **Assigned reading:** ISLR ch. 2

---

## 📊 Slides

- 🖥️ [Clean slides](slides_clean.pdf)
- 🖨️ [Slides (2-up)](slides_split.pdf)  *(two per page, for printing)*

## 📚 Readings

- 📄 [ISLR (selected pages)](readings/islr_class1.pdf)
- 📄 [Breimann (2001)](readings/reading1_breimann2001.pdf)
- 📄 [Gelman (2020)](readings/reading2_gelman2020.pdf)

## 💻 Code

**Figure scripts** — reproduce each figure from the slides:

- 📈 [fig1.R](code/fig1.R)
- 📈 [fig2.R](code/fig2.R)
- 📈 [fig3.R](code/fig3.R)
- 📈 [fig4.R](code/fig4.R)

## 🗂️ Data

- 📊 [breast_tumor_measurements.csv](data/breast_tumor_measurements.csv)
- 📊 [heart_failure_data.csv](data/heart_failure_data.csv)
- 📊 [neonatal_brain_volumes.csv](data/neonatal_brain_volumes.csv)
- 📊 [sbp_bmi_measurements.csv](data/sbp_bmi_measurements.csv)

## ⭐ Extras

Optional supplementary material:

- 📄 [Noisy Labels](extras/Extra_NoisyLabels.pdf)
